(4, 5-chōme)\* | | Shibaura-Kōnan Area | * [Daiba](/wiki/Odaiba "Odaiba")\* * [Kōnan](/wiki/K%C5%8Dnan,_Tokyo "Kōnan, Tokyo")\* * [Shibaura](/wiki/Shibaura "Shibaura")\* * [Kaigan](/wiki/Kaigan "Kaigan") (2, 3-chōme)\* | | [Location of Minato in Tokyo](/wiki/File:Minato-ku_in_Tokyo_Prefecture_Ja.svg "Location of Minato in Tokyo")

([Japanese](/wiki/Japanese_language "Japanese language"): レインボーブリッジ, [Hepburn](/wiki/Hepburn_romanization "Hepburn romanization"): *Reinbō Burijji*) is a [suspension bridge](/wiki/Suspension_bridge "Suspension bridge") crossing northern [Tokyo Bay](/wiki/Tokyo_Bay "Tokyo Bay") between [Shibaura](/wiki/Shibaura "Shibaura") Pier and the [Odaiba](/wiki/Odaiba "Odaiba") waterfront development in [Minato, Tokyo](/wiki/Minato,_Tokyo "Minato, Tokyo"), Japan. Its official name is the Tokyo Port Bridge (東京港連絡橋, *Tōkyō Kō Renrakukyō*). The English name "Rainbow Bridge" was chosen by public vote. The bridge carries road, rail and pedestrian traffic on two decks and is a prominent element of the Odaiba waterfront skyline.

[ArchiTravel – Kawasaki Heavy Industries](http://www.architravel.com/architravel/architects/kawasaki-heavy-industries/) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20160506014410/http://www.architravel.com/architravel/architects/kawasaki-heavy-industries/) 2016-05-06 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine") Retrieved October 4, 2015.

[Rainbow Bridge record broken, October 6, 2002.](http://www.japantimes.co.jp/2002/10/06/announcements/rainbow-bridge-record-broken/) *[Japan Times](/wiki/Japan_Times "Japan Times")*. Retrieved October 4, 2015.
["首都高速道路株式会社｜首都高の名所｜レインボーブリッジ"](http://www.shutoko.jp/service/spot/rainbowbridge/index.html). Retrieved 2009-12-26.
[Tokyo Bureau of Port and Harbour – Rainbow Bridge](http://www.kouwan.metro.tokyo.jp/jigyo/faq/rinkou/rainbowbridge/) Retrieved October 4, 2015. (in Japanese)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Bridge in Tokyo, Japan

| Rainbow Bridge   レインボーブリッジ | |

|  | |

| Carries | * 4 lanes ([Shuto Expressway](/wiki/Shuto_Expressway "Shuto Expressway") –  [Daiba Route](/wiki/Daiba_Route "Daiba Route"), upper deck) * 4 lanes (Tokyo Prefectural Route 482, lower deck) * 2 tracks ( [Yurikamome](/wiki/Yurikamome "Yurikamome") rapid transit, lower deck) * Pedestrian walkways (lower deck) |

The **Rainbow Bridge** ([Japanese](/wiki/Japanese_language "Japanese language"): レインボーブリッジ, [Hepburn](/wiki/Hepburn_romanization "Hepburn romanization"): *Reinbō Burijji*) is a [suspension bridge](/wiki/Suspension_bridge "Suspension bridge") crossing northern [Tokyo Bay](/wiki/Tokyo_Bay "Tokyo Bay") between [Shibaura](/wiki/Shibaura "Shibaura") Pier and the [Odaiba](/wiki/Odaiba "Odaiba") waterfront development in [Minato, Tokyo](/wiki/Minato,_Tokyo "Minato, Tokyo"), Japan. Its official name is the Tokyo Port Bridge (東京港連絡橋, *Tōkyō Kō Renrakukyō*). The English name "Rainbow Bridge" was chosen by public vote. The bridge carries road, rail and pedestrian traffic on two decks and is a prominent element of the Odaiba waterfront skyline.

## Design and construction

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rainbow_Bridge_(Tokyo)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Design and construction")]

The bridge was built by [Kawasaki Heavy Industries](/wiki/Kawasaki_Heavy_Industries "Kawasaki Heavy Industries").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Construction began in 1987 and was completed in 1993.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The bridge is a [double-decker bridge](/wiki/Bridge#Double-decked_bridges "Bridge") with an overall length of 798 m (2,618 ft) and a main span of 580 m (1,903 ft).[[4]](#cite_note-4) The towers supporting the bridge are painted white to harmonize with the skyline of central Tokyo as seen from Odaiba. Lamps placed on the supporting cables are illuminated in three different colors—red, white and green—each night using solar energy collected during the day.

## Usage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rainbow_Bridge_(Tokyo)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Usage")]

The Rainbow Bridge carries three transportation lines on two decks.

### Upper deck

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rainbow_Bridge_(Tokyo)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Upper deck")]

The upper deck carries the [Shuto Expressway](/wiki/Shuto_Expressway "Shuto Expressway")'s [Daiba Route](/wiki/Route_11_(Shuto_Expressway) "Route 11 (Shuto Expressway)").

### Lower deck

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rainbow_Bridge_(Tokyo)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Lower deck")]

The lower deck carries the [Yurikamome](/wiki/Yurikamome "Yurikamome") rapid transit line in the centre, Tokyo Prefectural Route 482 between the walkways and the road lanes, and pedestrian walkways on the outer sides.

#### Vehicles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rainbow_Bridge_(Tokyo)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Vehicles")]

Light motorcycles under 50 cc are not permitted on either deck or on the walkways. Motorcycle [pillion](/wiki/Pillion "Pillion") passengers are also banned.

#### Walkways

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rainbow_Bridge_(Tokyo)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Walkways")]

There are two separate pedestrian walkways on the north and south sides of the lower deck. The north walkway offers views of the inner Tokyo harbour and [Tokyo Tower](/wiki/Tokyo_Tower "Tokyo Tower"); the south walkway offers views of Tokyo Bay and, on clear days, [Mount Fuji](/wiki/Mount_Fuji "Mount Fuji"). Access hours are seasonal: 09:00–21:00 in summer and 10:00–18:00 in winter; access to the walkways closes 30 minutes before closing time.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Bicycles are permitted only when pushed (not ridden) because of the walkways' narrow width. The bridge can be reached on foot from [Tamachi Station](/wiki/Tamachi_Station_(Tokyo) "Tamachi Station (Tokyo)") ([JR East](/wiki/JR_East "JR East")) or [Shibaura-futō Station](/wiki/Shibaura-fut%C5%8D_Station "Shibaura-futō Station") ([Yurikamome](/wiki/Yurikamome "Yurikamome")) on the mainland side.

## Panorama

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rainbow_Bridge_(Tokyo)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Panorama")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/Rainbow_Bridge%2CTokyo_Bay_from_Odaiba.jpg/960px-Rainbow_Bridge%2CTokyo_Bay_from_Odaiba.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rainbow_Bridge,Tokyo_Bay_from_Odaiba.jpg "A panoramic view of Rainbow Bridge and the surrounding Tokyo Bay area as seen from Odaiba during the day")

A panoramic view of Rainbow Bridge and the surrounding Tokyo Bay area as seen from [Odaiba](/wiki/Odaiba "Odaiba") during the day

  View of the walkway

  Rainbow Bridge at night, lit in white lights

  Rainbow Bridge at night, lit in rainbow colors

## See also
